# NeuroEDA

<div align="center">

**An LLM-powered Exploratory Data Analysis agent — upload any CSV, get instant statistical insights, auto-generated visualizations, and a downloadable PDF report.**

</div>

---

## What is NeuroEDA?

Data Scientists and Analysts spend 40–60% of their time on Exploratory Data Analysis — writing the same profiling code for every new dataset, manually creating charts, manually writing summary reports. It's repetitive, it's tedious, and it's the same work every single time.

NeuroEDA automates the entire EDA pipeline. You upload a CSV file, click Analyze, and NeuroEDA runs a full statistical profile, generates visualizations automatically, passes the results to a NVIDIA-hosted Llama 3.1 API for AI-powered narrative insights, generates testable business hypotheses, and exports everything to a professionally formatted PDF report.

Zero code. Seconds, not hours.

---

## How It Works

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                   Gradio Web UI                      │
│                  (upload CSV file)                    │
└──────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────┘
                           │
                           ▼
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              Data Profiler                           │
│   Pandas + NumPy                                     │
│   — schema detection (dtypes, nulls, cardinality)    │
│   — distribution analysis (describe() stats)         │
│   — correlation matrix                               │
│   — duplicate row detection                          │
└──────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────┘
                           │
              ┌────────────┴────────────┐
              ▼                         ▼
┌──────────────────────┐    ┌────────────────────────────┐
│  Visualization       │    │  LLM Insight Generator     │
│  Engine (Plotly)     │    │  NVIDIA API + Llama 3.1 8B │
│  — histograms        │    │  — 5-section analysis      │
│  — correlation hmap  │    │  — pattern identification  │
│  — bar charts        │    │  — anomaly commentary      │
│  — scatter plot      │    │  — 5 business hypotheses   │
│    (vs target col)   │    └────────────────────────────┘
└──────────────────────┘                │
              │                         │
              └────────────┬────────────┘
                           ▼
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              PDF Report Generator                    │
│              fpdf2                                   │
│   — statistical summary                              │
│   — embedded chart images (kaleido export)           │
│   — AI narrative sections                            │
│   — hypothesis list                                  │
└──────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────┘
                           │
                           ▼
                  📄 Downloadable PDF Report
```

*This shows the logical data flow. In the actual pipeline the two LLM calls (insights,
then hypotheses) run one after another, followed by chart generation and finally the
PDF — everything is sequential, not parallelized.*

---

## Features

| Feature | Description |
|---|---|
|  Automated Statistical Profile | Schema detection, null analysis, distributions, cardinality, correlations, duplicate row count |
|  AI-Powered Insights | 5-section narrative analysis generated by NVIDIA Llama 3.1 8B, including LLM-flagged anomalies and outlier commentary based on the statistical profile |
|  Smart Visualizations | Histograms, correlation heatmap, bar charts, and a target-correlation scatter plot — auto-selected by data type |
|  Hypothesis Generation | 5 testable, domain-relevant business hypotheses per dataset |
|  PDF Report Export | Professionally formatted downloadable report with charts embedded |
|  No-Code Web UI | Gradio interface — upload a file and click Analyze, no Python knowledge needed |

> **Note:** Target-aware coloring and the scatter plot activate for datasets with a recognizable, numeric target column (`Survived`, `SalePrice`, or `target`, matched case-insensitively). Other datasets still get the full statistical profile, histograms, bar charts, and correlation heatmap.
>
> **Note:** To keep reports readable, the visualization engine caps itself at histograms for the first 6 numeric columns and bar charts for the first 3 low-cardinality categorical columns (≤15 unique values). On wide datasets (e.g. House Prices' 79 feature columns) this means only a subset gets its own chart — the full statistical profile and correlation heatmap still cover every numeric column.

---

## Tech Stack

| Library | Version | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `openai` | ≥1.0.0 | NVIDIA API client (compatible endpoint) |
| `pandas` | ≥1.5.0 | Data loading, cleaning, and statistical profiling |
| `numpy` | ≥1.23.0 | Numerical operations and array manipulation |
| `plotly` | ≥5.0.0 | Interactive and exportable chart generation |
| `statsmodels` | ≥0.14.0 | Powers the OLS trendline on the target-correlation scatter plot |
| `fpdf2` | ≥2.7.6 | PDF report generation and layout (incl. grid tables) |
| `gradio` | ≥4.0.0 | Web UI — file upload, output display (tested through 6.x) |
| `kaleido` | 0.2.1 | Exports Plotly charts to PNG for PDF embedding |

> **Note:** `kaleido` is pinned to `0.2.1` on purpose — later `1.x` releases changed the export API in a way that breaks `fig.write_image()` as used here. On Apple Silicon Macs, `0.2.1` can occasionally have install friction; if `pip install` fails for `kaleido` specifically, try `pip install kaleido==0.2.1 --no-cache-dir` or install it in a fresh virtual environment.

│

├── NeuroEDA-nvidia-api.ipynb     # Jupyter notebook — experimentation and prototyping
├── requirements.txt              # All Python dependencies

---

## Getting Started

### Prerequisites

- Python 3.10+
- An NVIDIA API key : get one free at [build.nvidia.com](https://build.nvidia.com)

### 1. Clone the repository

```bash
git clone https://github.com/ayushhh-here/NeuroEDA.git
cd NeuroEDA
```

### 2. Install dependencies

```bash
pip install -r requirements.txt
```

### 3. Set your NVIDIA API key

```bash
# Linux / macOS
export NVIDIA_API_KEY="nvapi-xxxxxxxxxxxx"

# Windows (PowerShell)
$env:NVIDIA_API_KEY="nvapi-xxxxxxxxxxxx"
```

### 4. Run the app

```bash
python app.py
```

Gradio will print a local URL — open it in your browser.

---

## How to Use

1. Open the Gradio UI in your browser
2. Upload any CSV file (tested with Titanic, House Prices, customer datasets)
3. Click **Analyze**
4. Review the statistical profile, charts, and AI insights rendered in the UI
5. Download the PDF report with the full analysis

---

## Datasets Tested

| Dataset | Rows | Columns | Domain |
|---|---|---|---|
| Titanic | 891 | 12 | Survival analysis - binary classification |
| House Prices (Kaggle) | 1,460 | 81 | Real estate - regression |

Both datasets are publicly available on Kaggle. Any well-formed CSV file will work; datasets without a `Survived`/`SalePrice`/`target` column will get the full profile and charts minus target-based coloring (see note under Features).

## LLM Integration — How It Works

NeuroEDA uses NVIDIA's API endpoint, which is OpenAI-compatible. The `openai` Python library makes the API call; you just swap the `base_url` to NVIDIA's endpoint and use your NVIDIA API key instead.

The model used is **Meta Llama 3.1 8B Instruct** ; a capable open-weight model that NVIDIA hosts and makes available with a free-tier API. The statistical profile from Pandas is serialized and injected into the prompt as context, so the model generates insights grounded in your actual data rather than hallucinating generic observations.

Each call generates:
- Overview of the dataset
- Key statistical observations
- Detected patterns and correlations
- Anomalies and outlier commentary (LLM-inferred from the statistical profile, not a separate statistical outlier-detection step)
- 5 testable business hypotheses

---

## Why I Built This

I'm targeting data analyst and data science roles. I wanted to build something that demonstrates the full pipeline — not just model training, but the messy real-world work that comes before it: understanding raw data, profiling it, visualizing it, and communicating findings.

This project gave me hands-on experience with:
- Building end-to-end LLM-powered pipelines (prompt engineering, structured output, API integration)
- Automated visualization — choosing the right chart type based on column dtype programmatically
- PDF report generation for business use cases
- Building usable AI data apps with Gradio that non-technical users can actually run

---

## Roadmap

- [ ] User-provided analysis goal (free-text prompt that conditions the LLM insights and hypotheses)
- [ ] Statistical (IQR / z-score) outlier detection in the profiler, not just LLM-inferred commentary
- [ ] Box plots and pairplots in the visualization engine
- [ ] User-selectable target column via dropdown (instead of a fixed name list)
- [ ] Support for Excel (.xlsx) file uploads
- [ ] Deploy on HuggingFace Spaces for public access
- [ ] Time-series analysis module with trend detection
- [ ] SQL database connection support
- [ ] Multi-dataset comparison and diff analysis
- [ ] Export to HTML report format
